factors.summary: Free cash flow generation has shown improvement, with increases in operating cash flows and positive trends contributing to overall growth. This has supported both operational activities and investments, as well as growth initiatives and shareholder returns.

evidence.text: Operating cash flows from continuing operations were $706 million during the first nine months of 2021, an increase of $58 million, or 9%, as compared to the comparable period of 2020.
Positive cash flow from operations is increasing, contributing to free cash flow growth.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Improvement in free cash flow supports growth and shareholder returns.

factors.summary: The company has demonstrated active and successful acquisition activity, with the ServiceChannel acquisition being accretive and contributing to both sales growth and margin expansion. The investing cash flows increased due to this acquisition, despite a decrease in capital expenditures compared to the previous year.

evidence.text: The increase in investing cash flows was due to the $1.2 billion acquisition of ServiceChannel that was partially offset by a year-over-year decrease in capital expenditures of $31 million largely due to higher spending in the comparable period in 2020 related to the integration of the ASP business.
Acquisitions contributed to sales growth and margin expansion through new capabilities.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Successful acquisition activity contributing to growth and margin expansion.

factors.summary: The company is improving its debt leverage through deleveraging and refinancing activities. There is evidence of lower average debt balances and decreased net interest expense.

evidence.text: The company has been managing its debt by repaying and refinancing, leading to delevering.

We have generally satisfied any short-term liquidity needs that are not met through operating cash flows and available cash through issuances of commercial paper under our U.S. dollar and Euro-denominated commercial paper programs... In August 2021, we resumed borrowing under our Commercial Paper Program to fund, in part, the ServiceChannel acquisition.
Net interest expense decreased due to lower average debt balances.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Improving debt leverage through deleveraging and refinancing.

factors.summary: The order backlog is growing due to supply chain challenges, with demand outpacing delivery capabilities and new orders outpacing revenue growth.

evidence.text: Order backlog is growing significantly due to supply chain challenges, with demand outpacing delivery capabilities.
Components of Sales Growth: Total revenue growth (GAAP) was 16.6% and 18.6% for the three and nine month periods ended October 1, 2021, respectively.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The growing order backlog indicates strong demand and potential future revenue, despite supply chain challenges.

factors.summary: The supply chain has shown improvement with normalization of lead times and reduced disruptions, but continues to face challenges such as labor and material shortages, impacting production and costs.

evidence.text: The year-over-year increase in cost of sales was partially offset by productivity improvement initiatives, indicating supply chain normalization.
Due to the volatility and disruption in the commercial paper markets during the first six months of 2020, we temporarily reduced our reliance on this source of funding...

During the third quarter of 2021, widespread supply chain challenges due to labor, raw material, and component shortages as well as logistics issues with port congestion affected multiple industries and raised material and shipping costs. Our supply chain was largely responsive to these dynamics and we successfully implemented solutions to effectively support our near-term operations, countermeasure production material shortages and distribution limitations, and manage product margins to drive year-over-year growth and market share gains in the third quarter of 2021.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: While there have been improvements and implemented solutions, ongoing challenges like labor and material shortages still impact production and costs.

factors.summary: The overall demand for products and services has been accelerating, supported by increased sales from existing businesses and strong demand across multiple end markets. The easing of virus control measures and the distribution of vaccinations in developed countries have contributed to this positive momentum. Year-over-year sales growth of 13.1% and 14.7% during the three and nine month periods ended October 1, 2021, respectively, further highlights the robust demand trends observed in the market.

evidence.text: Overall demand for our products and services has continued to accelerate during the three and nine month periods ended October 1, 2021, as the distribution and administration of vaccinations in developed countries has led to easing of virus control measures.

Year-over-year sales of products and services from existing businesses increased 13.1% and 14.7% during the three and nine month periods ended October 1, 2021, respectively, as compared to the comparable periods of 2020.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Accelerating demand across multiple end markets, supported by easing virus measures and strong sales growth, indicates a robust market position.

factors.summary: Management has maintained forward guidance, indicating confidence in future performance. However, specific updates were not provided in the critical accounting estimates section.

evidence.text: Management has not revised forward guidance, reflecting confidence in continued growth and margin expansion.

We expect year-over-year revenue from sales of our products and services to increase between 6.5% and 8.5% in the fourth quarter of 2021 and between 14% and 14.5% for the year ending December 31, 2021, based on our current assumptions about easing global virus control measures, continued recovery of certain end markets, elective surgery rates remaining below pre-pandemic levels, the availability of electronic components and our ability to convert backlog, and sustaining workforce levels and manufacturing capacity.

There were no material changes during the three and nine month periods ended October 1, 2021 to the items we disclosed as our critical accounting estimates in 'Management’s Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations' in our 2020 Annual Report on Form 10-K.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Management's maintained forward guidance reflects confidence in future growth and performance, despite not providing specific updates.

factors.summary: The company demonstrates strong pricing power, as evidenced by successful price increases contributing to sales growth across different segments. The price increases outpaced material cost increases in the short term, indicating effective cost management alongside revenue enhancement.

evidence.text: Year-over-year price increases contributed 2.2% and 1.5% to sales growth during the three and nine month periods ended October 1, 2021, respectively, as compared to the comparable periods of 2020, and is reflected as a component of the change in sales from existing businesses. For the three month period ended October 1, 2021, price increases outpaced material cost increases.
Year-over-year price increases in our Intelligent Operating Solutions segment contributed 2.1% and 1.6% to sales growth during the three and nine month periods ended October 1, 2021, respectively.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Strong pricing power with price increases outpacing material costs shows effective revenue enhancement and cost management.

factors.summary: The revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin segments, particularly in short-cycle industrial and SaaS businesses, which is driving growth and improving overall margins.

evidence.text: Sales from existing businesses increased 9.1% and 12.9% during the three and nine month periods ended October 1, 2021, respectively, as compared to the comparable periods of 2020, reflecting positive, broad-based momentum and focused execution across our portfolio, most notably within our short-cycle industrial and SAAS businesses.
Operating profit margin increased 380 basis points during the nine month period ended October 1, 2021 as compared to the comparable period of 2020.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Shifting revenue mix towards higher-margin segments improves overall margins and drives growth.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is accelerating, driven by strong performance across existing businesses and favorable economic trends. Additionally, there is strong demand across end markets contributing to this growth.

evidence.text: Sales from existing businesses increased 9.1% and 12.9% during the three and nine month periods ended October 1, 2021, respectively, as compared to the comparable periods of 2020, reflecting positive, broad-based momentum and focused execution across our portfolio.

Year-over-year sales of products and services from existing businesses of Intelligent Operating Solutions increased 13.1% and 14.7% during the three and nine month periods ended October 1, 2021, respectively.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Accelerating organic growth across existing businesses and favorable economic trends indicate strong market performance.

factors.summary: Unit volumes are growing, driven by increased demand and effective supply chain management across various regions, including high growth and developed markets, Latin America, and Asia. The growth is attributed to real demand rather than price increases, supported by favorable sales mix and effective management strategies.

evidence.text: Year-over-year sales from existing businesses in both high growth and developed markets increased at a high single-digit rate during the three month period ended October 1, 2021, driven by high single-digit growth in North America and Europe, greater than 20% growth in Latin America, and mid-single-digit growth in Asia.
Year-over-year sales volumes from existing businesses, price increases, and a favorable sales mix contributed to revenue growth.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Growth in unit volumes across various regions, driven by real demand and effective management, supports positive volume trends.

factors.summary: The end market momentum is positive across multiple regions and industries, with strong growth in high-growth regions like Latin America, mid-teens growth in Europe, low double-digit growth in North America and Asia, and growth in industrial, healthcare, and utilities sectors.

evidence.text: Year-over-year sales from existing businesses in developed and high growth markets increased at a low double-digit rate and at a rate in the mid-teens, respectively, driven by low double-digit growth in North America and Asia, growth at a rate in the mid-teens in Europe, and greater than 20% growth in Latin America.

The businesses in our Intelligent Operating Solutions segment provide a broad and differentiated offering of instrumentation, sensors, software, and services to address these critical workflows for our customers.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Strong growth across regions and industries indicates robust market demand.

factors.summary: The company is facing rising input costs, particularly in materials and freight, but is managing these increases through pricing adjustments and productivity improvements. While the situation presents challenges, the implemented measures indicate a strategic response to mitigate cost pressures.

evidence.text: Raw material and component costs are rising, but the company is offsetting these through pricing and productivity improvements.
Higher year-over-year material and freight costs were partially offset by restructuring and productivity improvement initiatives.

Further, we have increased the employee capacity at the majority of our facilities in compliance with local COVID-19 guidelines and have implemented additional safety and space management protocols to help ensure the health and safety of our employees. We plan to be in compliance with vaccine mandates, including the September 9, 2021 Executive Order on Ensuring Adequate COVID Safety Protocols for Federal Contractors (the 'Executive Order'), to the extent applicable to us.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: While facing rising costs, the company is taking strategic steps to mitigate them, resulting in mixed signals.

factors.summary: Restructuring and productivity initiatives are delivering measurable savings, contributing to margin expansion. However, these savings are partially offset by higher employee compensation costs, SG&A expenses, and freight logistics costs.

evidence.text: Year-over-year operating profit margin comparisons were favorably impacted by: Higher year-over-year sales volumes from existing businesses, price increases, and to a lesser extent, incremental year-over-year cost savings associated with restructuring and productivity improvement initiatives, which were partially offset by higher year-over-year employee compensation costs and higher selling, general, and administrative expenses ('SG&A costs') due to broad cost reduction efforts in the comparable period of 2020 that reduced expenses to better align with reductions in demand and higher freight and logistics costs — favorable 325 basis points
Incremental year-over-year cost savings associated with restructuring and productivity improvement initiatives were partially offset by higher costs.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Restructuring and productivity initiatives are delivering measurable savings, contributing to margin expansion.

factors.summary: Operating and gross margins are expanding, driven by higher sales volumes, price increases, and cost savings initiatives. However, there are some pressures from higher compensation and logistics costs.

evidence.text: Operating profit margin was 15.5% for the three month period ended October 1, 2021, an increase of 360 basis points as compared to 11.9% in the comparable period of 2020.

Year-over-year operating profit margin comparisons were favorably impacted by: Higher year-over-year sales volumes from existing businesses, price increases, and to a lesser extent, incremental year-over-year cost savings associated with restructuring and productivity improvement initiatives, which were partially offset by higher year-over-year employee compensation costs and higher selling, general, and administrative expenses ('SG&A costs') due to broad cost reduction efforts in the comparable period of 2020 that reduced expenses to better align with reductions in demand and higher freight and logistics costs — favorable 325 basis points
Operating profit margin increased 380 basis points during the nine month period ended October 1, 2021 as compared to the comparable period of 2020.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Margins are expanding due to higher sales, price increases, and cost savings, despite some cost pressures.

factors.summary: Geopolitical risks, particularly related to U.S.-China relations and trade policies, are being monitored, but their specific impacts on financial results are not yet quantifiable.

evidence.text: We are also continuing to monitor any escalation of geopolitical uncertainties related to governmental policies toward international trade, monetary and fiscal policies, relations between the U.S. and China, and we are continuing to evaluate the impacts of proposed and recently enacted legislation on our business and the potential impacts of various investment and taxation policy initiatives in the United States and by the Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development. As of the filing date of this report, we are unable to quantify the anticipated impacts on our financial results for the year ending December 31, 2021, if any, and any impact in future periods, and will continue monitoring these developments.
No significant geopolitical impacts were reported in the financial data provided.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Geopolitical risks are being monitored, but their financial impact is currently unquantifiable, leading to mixed signals.

factors.summary: Key sensitivities include supply chain challenges, inflationary pressures, and the impact of new COVID-19 variants on demand and virus control measures.

evidence.text: While we expect the positive, broad-based momentum in demand across our portfolio to continue in the fourth quarter of 2021, we will continue deploying and applying the Fortive Business System to help drive near-term performance by actively managing our supply chain and continuing to collaborate with our customers and suppliers to minimize anticipated material and component delays and production and logistics disruptions. We expect these supply chain challenges and inflationary pressures will continue during the fourth quarter of 2021; however, we believe we will largely offset these inflationary cost pressures with price increases and other countermeasures.
Higher material and freight costs, along with foreign currency fluctuations, impact the company's margins.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Sensitivities include supply chain and inflationary pressures, but also opportunities to offset these challenges.
